target_text: Hey, I wanted to leave a fuller note so we can hear how the voice holds up over a longer stretch. The short samples are useful for latency, but they do not tell us much about consistency, pacing, breathiness, or how well the model carries emotion from one sentence to the next. So this version is intentionally longer and a little more conversational. It moves through a few different rhythms, includes some pauses, and should make it easier to notice whether the tone stays stable or starts to drift. I also want enough material to expose awkward transitions, repeated prosody, or the sort of flattening that sometimes shows up once a model has been speaking for a while. If the identity stays recognizable, the phrasing remains clear, and the overall delivery still sounds like one coherent person, then we will know it is much closer to something we could actually use in a real call. That is the point of this run.
